privileged_info_summary: The Pima Indians Diabetes dataset is real data from the National Institute of Diabetes and Digestive and Kidney Diseases, containing 768 records of Pima Indian women aged 21+. The dataset has two biologically implausible zero values: 5 zeros in the Glucose column (plasma glucose concentration) and 11 zeros in the BMI column. The expected ROC AUC values for using Glucose to predict diabetes outcome are 0.79, and for BMI are 0.69. Column names are 'Glucose' (2-hour plasma glucose concentration) and 'BMI' (body mass index). The raw dataset is available from jbrownlee's public GitHub repository.
